institution: Sector-wide typology - Indian retail digital payments
source_documents: https://www.rbi.org.in (Master Direction - Digital Payment Security Controls, Feb 2021)
risk_signals: A new device is linked to the wallet, or wallet KYC/registered number is changed
Rapid drain of wallet balance and any linked bank account or card
Takeover often preceded by SIM swap, OTP phishing, or credential compromise
analyst_reasoning: A wallet consolidates balance plus linked funding sources, so taking it over yields immediate liquidity. Attackers gain entry through the usual vectors (SIM swap, OTP phishing) then link a new device and drain fast before alerts are acted on. The new-device-link event is the pivot.
investigation_steps: Review device-link history and KYC/number changes on the wallet
Trace the drain path from wallet balance to linked bank/card and onward beneficiaries
Identify the entry vector (SIM swap, phishing, malware) from surrounding events
decision: Freeze the wallet, unlink the rogue device, break links to bank/card, and re-verify the customer before restoring access.
outcome: Wallets are drained within minutes of takeover; loss size depends on balance and linked-account limits.
lessons_learned: An unrecognised device-link or KYC change is the earliest sign of wallet takeover
Wallets inherit the risk of every funding source linked to them
Fast unlinking of the rogue device is the key containment step
